description: Initialize project with Cadre context-driven development

preconditions.requirement: Every project-scoped packet call includes root
If a required packet returns ok:false, halt and report the packet error

packetFlow.instruction: Call `cadre_project` with `action: "ping"` to confirm MCP availability. If the client asks for repeated approval on Cadre MCP tools, tell the user to rerun `cadre install`; the installer bootstraps Cadre-only MCP approval rules for Codex and Claude without approving shell commands or non-Cadre tools.

Before the first setup packet, scan repository evidence such as README/docs, manifests, source entrypoints, schemas, tests, and commands. Then call `cadre_workflow` with `workflow: "setup"`, the root candidate, and full structured `product`, `productGuidelines`, `workflowPolicy`, and `techStack` JSON fields. Do not pass only summary/goals/repoNotes unless the user explicitly requested minimal setup.

Use the returned doctor report, workspace diagnostics, dependency graph, LSP evidence, template inventory, selected JSON style guides, and any `intent_prompts` or `native_prompts`. When prompts are present, load the `native-prompts` reference and answer those prompts first with the client native selection affordance before review-bundle approval.

After prompt answers have been mapped back to structured setup arguments, review only `approval.current_stage` using the returned review_bundle manifest/path list. When the user approves that single stage, call setup again with `approvalSessionId`, `approvalStage` set to the current stage, and `approvedStages` containing exactly one additional stage in order. Do not inline large generated artifacts into model context.

When the packet reports all setup stages approved, call `cadre_workflow` again with `workflow: "setup"`, `execute: true`, `approvalComplete: true`, and the same `approvalSessionId` using the same structured payload.
Summarize created canonical JSON/JSONL files, generated human projections, selected code style guides, native state initialization, warnings, and next packet.

forbiddenActions: Do not recreate Cadre control-plane, native event/message state, index, worktree, provider, merge, or cleanup state with shell commands
Do not read or write Markdown projections as canonical state
Do not use Markdown payload fields, legacy Markdown import, or prompt-side state repair

product.name: Project name from repo evidence
product.summary: Concrete product behavior implemented by this repository.
coreWorkflows: Create or ingest a domain object
Process, review, publish, or recover it
invariants: Stable behavior that must not regress
